reasoning_content: We need to check if 389 is prime. Let's test divisibility. It's odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits 3+8+9=20, not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*55=385, remainder 4, so not divisible by 7. 11: 11*35=385, remainder 4, not divisible. 13: 13*29=377, remainder 12; 13*30=390, so no. 17: 17*22=374, remainder 15; 17*23=391, so no. 19: 19*20=380, remainder 9; 19*21=399, so no. Next prime 23: 23*16=368, remainder 21; 23*17=391, so no. 29: 29*13=377, remainder 12; 29*14=406, too high. 31: 31*12=372, remainder 17; 31*13=403. 37: 37*10=370, remainder 19; 37*11=407. 41: 41*9=369, remainder 20; 41*10=410. 43: 43*9=387, remainder 2; not divisible. sqrt(389) is about 19.72, actually sqrt(389) ≈ 19.72, so we only need to check primes up to 19. Already checked 2,3,5,7,11,13,17,19. None divide. So 389 is prime. Answer: Yes.
